steps.title: Identify the faces
steps.content: A triangular pyramid has $4$ triangular faces. Since all $6$ edges are $84$ centimeters long, each face is an equilateral triangle with side length $84$.

steps.title: Find the surface area
steps.content:
The area of one face is
$$
\frac{84^2\sqrt{3}}{4}
$$
With $4$ congruent faces, the total surface area is
$$
4\bigl(\frac{84^2\sqrt{3}}{4}\bigr)=84^2\sqrt{3}
$$

steps.title: Read the coefficient
steps.content: Compute the coefficient of $\sqrt{3}$. $$ 84^2=7{,}056 $$ So the surface area is $7{,}056\sqrt{3}$, which means $k=7{,}056$. The answer is 7056.
